attributes #0 = { nocallback nofree nosync nounwind speculatable willreturn memory(none) }

define <4 x i32> @PR42100(<4 x i32> noundef %x, <4 x i32> noundef %min) {
; CHECK-LABEL: @PR42100(
; CHECK-NEXT:  entry:
; CHECK-NEXT:    [[TMP0:%.*]] = tail call <4 x i32> @llvm.smin.v4i32(<4 x i32> [[X:%.*]], <4 x i32> [[MIN:%.*]])
; CHECK-NEXT:    ret <4 x i32> [[TMP0]]
entry:
  br label %for.cond

for.cond:                                         ; preds = %for.inc, %entry
  %min.addr.0 = phi <4 x i32> [ %min, %entry ], [ %min.addr.1, %for.inc ]
  %i.0 = phi i32 [ 0, %entry ], [ %inc, %for.inc ]
  %cmp = icmp ne i32 %i.0, 4
  br i1 %cmp, label %for.body, label %for.cond.cleanup

for.cond.cleanup:                                 ; preds = %for.cond
  br label %for.end

for.body:                                         ; preds = %for.cond
  %vecext = extractelement <4 x i32> %x, i32 %i.0
  %vecext1 = extractelement <4 x i32> %min.addr.0, i32 %i.0
  %cmp2 = icmp slt i32 %vecext, %vecext1
  br i1 %cmp2, label %if.then, label %if.end

if.then:                                          ; preds = %for.body
  %vecext3 = extractelement <4 x i32> %x, i32 %i.0
  %vecins = insertelement <4 x i32> %min.addr.0, i32 %vecext3, i32 %i.0
  br label %if.end

if.end:                                           ; preds = %if.then, %for.body
  %min.addr.1 = phi <4 x i32> [ %vecins, %if.then ], [ %min.addr.0, %for.body ]
  br label %for.inc

for.inc:                                          ; preds = %if.end
  %inc = add nsw i32 %i.0, 1
  br label %for.cond

for.end:                                          ; preds = %for.cond.cleanup
  ret <4 x i32> %min.addr.0
}
tests.source_program:
define <4 x i32> @PR42100(<4 x i32> noundef %x, <4 x i32> noundef %min) {
entry:
  br label %for.cond

for.cond:                                         ; preds = %for.inc, %entry
  %min.addr.0 = phi <4 x i32> [ %min, %entry ], [ %min.addr.1, %for.inc ]
  %i.0 = phi i32 [ 0, %entry ], [ %inc, %for.inc ]
  %cmp = icmp ne i32 %i.0, 4
  br i1 %cmp, label %for.body, label %for.cond.cleanup

for.cond.cleanup:                                 ; preds = %for.cond
  br label %for.end

for.body:                                         ; preds = %for.cond
  %vecext = extractelement <4 x i32> %x, i32 %i.0
  %vecext1 = extractelement <4 x i32> %min.addr.0, i32 %i.0
  %cmp2 = icmp slt i32 %vecext, %vecext1
  br i1 %cmp2, label %if.then, label %if.end

if.then:                                          ; preds = %for.body
  %vecext3 = extractelement <4 x i32> %x, i32 %i.0
  %vecins = insertelement <4 x i32> %min.addr.0, i32 %vecext3, i32 %i.0
  br label %if.end

if.end:                                           ; preds = %if.then, %for.body
  %min.addr.1 = phi <4 x i32> [ %vecins, %if.then ], [ %min.addr.0, %for.body ]
  br label %for.inc

for.inc:                                          ; preds = %if.end
  %inc = add nsw i32 %i.0, 1
  br label %for.cond

for.end:                                          ; preds = %for.cond.cleanup
  ret <4 x i32> %min.addr.0
}

tests.test_body:
define <4 x i8> @allSignBits_vec(<4 x i8> %cond, <4 x i8> %tval, <4 x i8> %fval) {
; CHECK-LABEL: @allSignBits_vec(
; CHECK-NEXT:    [[ISNEG1:%.*]] = icmp slt <4 x i8> [[COND:%.*]], zeroinitializer
; CHECK-NEXT:    [[SEL:%.*]] = select <4 x i1> [[ISNEG1]], <4 x i8> [[TVAL:%.*]], <4 x i8> [[FVAL:%.*]]
; CHECK-NEXT:    ret <4 x i8> [[SEL]]
  %bitmask = ashr <4 x i8> %cond, <i8 7, i8 7, i8 7, i8 7>
  %not_bitmask = xor <4 x i8> %bitmask, <i8 -1, i8 -1, i8 -1, i8 -1>
  %a1 = and <4 x i8> %tval, %bitmask
  %a2 = and <4 x i8> %fval, %not_bitmask
  %sel = or <4 x i8> %a2, %a1
  ret <4 x i8> %sel
}
tests.source_program:
define <4 x i8> @allSignBits_vec(<4 x i8> %cond, <4 x i8> %tval, <4 x i8> %fval) {
  %bitmask = ashr <4 x i8> %cond, <i8 7, i8 7, i8 7, i8 7>
  %not_bitmask = xor <4 x i8> %bitmask, <i8 -1, i8 -1, i8 -1, i8 -1>
  %a1 = and <4 x i8> %tval, %bitmask
  %a2 = and <4 x i8> %fval, %not_bitmask
  %sel = or <4 x i8> %a2, %a1
  ret <4 x i8> %sel
}

issue.title: [SLPVectorizer] Failure to use vectorize integer minimum loop
issue.body:
|  |  |
| --- | --- |
| Bugzilla Link | [42755](https://llvm.org/bz42755) |
| Version | trunk |
| OS | Windows NT |
| CC | @alexey-bataev,@anton-afanasyev,@dtemirbulatov,@LebedevRI,@rotateright,@vporpo |

## Extended Description 
Current codegen: https://gcc.godbolt.org/z/smAJKe
```
#include <x86intrin.h>

auto minmaxi(__v4si x, __v4si min) {
    for (int i = 0; i != 4; ++i)
      if (x[i] < min[i])
        min[i] = x[i];
    return min;
}
```
```
_Z7minmaxiDv4_iS_:
        vmovd   %xmm0, %eax
        vmovd   %xmm1, %ecx
        cmpl    %ecx, %eax
        jl      .LBB0_1
        vpextrd $1, %xmm0, %eax
        vpextrd $1, %xmm1, %ecx
        cmpl    %ecx, %eax
        jl      .LBB0_3
.LBB0_4:
        vpextrd $2, %xmm0, %eax
        vpextrd $2, %xmm1, %ecx
        cmpl    %ecx, %eax
        jl      .LBB0_5
.LBB0_6:
        vpextrd $3, %xmm0, %eax
        vpextrd $3, %xmm1, %ecx
        cmpl    %ecx, %eax
        jl      .LBB0_7
.LBB0_8:
        vmovdqa %xmm1, %xmm0
        retq
.LBB0_1:
        vpblendw        $3, %xmm0, %xmm1, %xmm1 # xmm1 = xmm0[0,1],xmm1[2,3,4,5,6,7]
        vpextrd $1, %xmm0, %eax
        vpextrd $1, %xmm1, %ecx
        cmpl    %ecx, %eax
        jge     .LBB0_4
.LBB0_3:
        vpblendw        $12, %xmm0, %xmm1, %xmm1 # xmm1 = xmm1[0,1],xmm0[2,3],xmm1[4,5,6,7]
        vpextrd $2, %xmm0, %eax
        vpextrd $2, %xmm1, %ecx
        cmpl    %ecx, %eax
        jge     .LBB0_6
.LBB0_5:
        vpblendw        $48, %xmm0, %xmm1, %xmm1 # xmm1 = xmm1[0,1,2,3],xmm0[4,5],xmm1[6,7]
        vpextrd $3, %xmm0, %eax
        vpextrd $3, %xmm1, %ecx
        cmpl    %ecx, %eax
        jge     .LBB0_8
.LBB0_7:
        vpblendw        $192, %xmm0, %xmm1, %xmm1 # xmm1 = xmm1[0,1,2,3,4,5],xmm0[6,7]
        vmovdqa %xmm1, %xmm0
        retq
```

comments.author: RKSimon
comments.body:
Wow:
```
define <4 x i32> @minmaxi(<4 x i32> %0, <4 x i32> %1) {
  %3 = icmp slt <4 x i32> %0, %1
  %4 = select <4 x i1> %3, <4 x i32> %0, <4 x i32> %1
  %5 = shufflevector <4 x i32> %4, <4 x i32> %1, <4 x i32> <i32 0, i32 5, i32 6, i32 7>
  %6 = icmp slt <4 x i32> %0, %1
  %7 = select <4 x i1> %6, <4 x i32> %0, <4 x i32> %5
  %8 = shufflevector <4 x i32> %5, <4 x i32> %7, <4 x i32> <i32 0, i32 5, i32 2, i32 3>
  %9 = icmp slt <4 x i32> %0, %1
  %10 = select <4 x i1> %9, <4 x i32> %0, <4 x i32> %8
  %11 = shufflevector <4 x i32> %8, <4 x i32> %10, <4 x i32> <i32 0, i32 1, i32 6, i32 3>
  %12 = icmp slt <4 x i32> %0, %1
  %13 = select <4 x i1> %12, <4 x i32> %0, <4 x i32> %11
  %14 = shufflevector <4 x i32> %11, <4 x i32> %13, <4 x i32> <i32 0, i32 1, i32 2, i32 7>
  ret <4 x i32> %14
}

minmaxi:
        vpminsd %xmm1, %xmm0, %xmm3
        vpcmpgtd        %xmm0, %xmm1, %xmm2
        vpblendw        $3, %xmm3, %xmm1, %xmm1         # xmm1 = xmm3[0,1],xmm1[2,3,4,5,6,7]
        vblendvps       %xmm2, %xmm0, %xmm1, %xmm3
        vblendps        $2, %xmm3, %xmm1, %xmm1         # xmm1 = xmm1[0],xmm3[1],xmm1[2,3]
        vblendvps       %xmm2, %xmm0, %xmm1, %xmm3
        vblendps        $4, %xmm3, %xmm1, %xmm1         # xmm1 = xmm1[0,1],xmm3[2],xmm1[3]
        vblendvps       %xmm2, %xmm0, %xmm1, %xmm0
        vblendps        $8, %xmm0, %xmm1, %xmm0         # xmm0 = xmm1[0,1,2],xmm0[3]
        retq
```
So we match the smin but still perform a load of completely useless shuffles/selects afterward!

comments.author: rotateright
comments.body:
That might be 2 independent transforms:
1. Reduce select condition based on vector demanded elements of a vector select.
2. Canonicalize order of select and select-shuffle (blend).

comments.author: rotateright
comments.body:
Alive2 is back!
http://volta.cs.utah.edu:8080/z/kUotJv

We're missing this select+shuf fold in instcombine:
```
define <4 x i8> @src(<4 x i8> %x, <4 x i8> %min, <4 x i1> %cmp) {
  %vecins = shufflevector <4 x i8> %x, <4 x i8> %min, <4 x i32> <i32 0, i32 5, i32 6, i32 7>
  %splat = shufflevector <4 x i1> %cmp, <4 x i1> undef, <4 x i32> zeroinitializer
  %r = select <4 x i1> %splat, <4 x i8> %vecins, <4 x i8> %min
  ret <4 x i8> %r
}

define <4 x i8> @tgt(<4 x i8> %x, <4 x i8> %min, <4 x i1> %cmp) {
  %r = select <4 x i1> %cmp, <4 x i8> %x, <4 x i8> %min
  %blend = shufflevector <4 x i8> %r, <4 x i8> %min, <4 x i32> <i32 0, i32 5, i32 6, i32 7>
  ret <4 x i8> %blend
}
```

comments.author: RKSimon
comments.body:
We're no longer branching, but we still don't create a regression pattern:

clang -g0 -O3 -march=btver2
```
define <4 x i32> @minmax(<4 x i32> %0, <4 x i32> %1) {
  %3 = icmp slt <4 x i32> %0, %1
  %4 = shufflevector <4 x i32> %0, <4 x i32> %1, <4 x i32> <i32 0, i32 5, i32 6, i32 7>
  %5 = shufflevector <4 x i1> %3, <4 x i1> undef, <4 x i32> zeroinitializer
  %6 = select <4 x i1> %5, <4 x i32> %4, <4 x i32> %1
  %7 = icmp sgt <4 x i32> %6, %0
  %8 = shufflevector <4 x i32> %6, <4 x i32> %0, <4 x i32> <i32 0, i32 5, i32 2, i32 3>
  %9 = shufflevector <4 x i1> %7, <4 x i1> undef, <4 x i32> <i32 1, i32 1, i32 1, i32 1>
  %10 = select <4 x i1> %9, <4 x i32> %8, <4 x i32> %6
  %11 = icmp sgt <4 x i32> %10, %0
  %12 = shufflevector <4 x i32> %10, <4 x i32> %0, <4 x i32> <i32 0, i32 1, i32 6, i32 3>
  %13 = shufflevector <4 x i1> %11, <4 x i1> undef, <4 x i32> <i32 2, i32 2, i32 2, i32 2>
  %14 = select <4 x i1> %13, <4 x i32> %12, <4 x i32> %10
  %15 = icmp sgt <4 x i32> %14, %0
  %16 = shufflevector <4 x i32> %14, <4 x i32> %0, <4 x i32> <i32 0, i32 1, i32 2, i32 7>
  %17 = shufflevector <4 x i1> %15, <4 x i1> undef, <4 x i32> <i32 3, i32 3, i32 3, i32 3>
  %18 = select <4 x i1> %17, <4 x i32> %16, <4 x i32> %14
  ret <4 x i32> %18
}

minmax:
        vpcmpgtd        %xmm0, %xmm1, %xmm2
        vpblendw        $3, %xmm0, %xmm1, %xmm3 # xmm3 = xmm0[0,1],xmm1[2,3,4,5,6,7]
        vpshufd $0, %xmm2, %xmm2        # xmm2 = xmm2[0,0,0,0]
        vblendvps       %xmm2, %xmm3, %xmm1, %xmm1
        vpcmpgtd        %xmm0, %xmm1, %xmm2
        vpblendw        $12, %xmm0, %xmm1, %xmm3 # xmm3 = xmm1[0,1],xmm0[2,3],xmm1[4,5,6,7]
        vpshufd $85, %xmm2, %xmm2       # xmm2 = xmm2[1,1,1,1]
        vblendvps       %xmm2, %xmm3, %xmm1, %xmm1
        vpcmpgtd        %xmm0, %xmm1, %xmm2
        vpblendw        $48, %xmm0, %xmm1, %xmm3 # xmm3 = xmm1[0,1,2,3],xmm0[4,5],xmm1[6,7]
        vpshufd $170, %xmm2, %xmm2      # xmm2 = xmm2[2,2,2,2]
        vblendvps       %xmm2, %xmm3, %xmm1, %xmm1
        vpcmpgtd        %xmm0, %xmm1, %xmm2
        vpblendw        $192, %xmm0, %xmm1, %xmm0 # xmm0 = xmm1[0,1,2,3,4,5],xmm0[6,7]
        vpshufd $255, %xmm2, %xmm2      # xmm2 = xmm2[3,3,3,3]
        vblendvps       %xmm2, %xmm0, %xmm1, %xmm0
        retq
```

comments.author: rotateright
comments.body:
Independent of what we do here, the extract vs. splat variation seems like something instcombine can take care of for us:
https://reviews.llvm.org/D66095

comments.author: rotateright
comments.body:
In the most minimal form, I think we want to do this:
```
define i1 @ext_cmp(<4 x i32> %x, <4 x i32> %y) {
  %x0 = extractelement <4 x i32> %x, i32 0
  %y0 = extractelement <4 x i32> %y, i32 0
  %cond = icmp slt i32 %x0, %y0
  ret i1 %cond
}

define i1 @ext_cmp(<4 x i32> %x, <4 x i32> %y) {
  %veccond = icmp slt <4 x i32> %x, %y
  %cond = extractelement <4 x i1> %veccond, i32 0
  ret i1 %cond
}
```
So the cost calculation is:
(2 extracts + scalar cmp) < (vector cmp + extract)

comments.author: rotateright
comments.body:
```
define <4 x i32> @ext_cmp(<4 x i32> %x, <4 x i32> %y, <4 x i32> %z, <4 x i32> %w) {
  %x0 = extractelement <4 x i32> %x, i32 0
  %y0 = extractelement <4 x i32> %y, i32 0
  %cond = icmp slt i32 %x0, %y0
  %r = select i1 %cond, <4 x i32> %z, <4 x i32> %w
  ret <4 x i32> %r
}
```

Can we enhance SLP to convert this to:
```
define <4 x i32> @ext_cmp2(<4 x i32> %x, <4 x i32> %y, <4 x i32> %z, <4 x i32> %w) {
  %condv = icmp slt <4 x i32> %x, %y
  %cond = extractelement <4 x i1> %condvec, i32 0
  %r = select i1 %cond, <4 x i32> %z, <4 x i32> %w
  ret <4 x i32> %r
}
```
or:
```
define <4 x i32> @ext_cmp3(<4 x i32> %x, <4 x i32> %y, <4 x i32> %z, <4 x i32> %w) {
  %condv = icmp slt <4 x i32> %x, %y
  %splatcond = shufflevector <4 x i1> %condv, <4 x i1> undef, <4 x i32> zeroinitializer
  %r = select <4 x i1> %splatcond, <4 x i32> %z, <4 x i32> %w
  ret <4 x i32> %r
}
```
That is, we want to recognize that a vector compare + splat + vector select is cheaper than 2 extracts + scalar compare + scalar select-of-vectors.

We are not allowed to create the vector compare in instcombine because there is no cost model there.

comments.author: rotateright
comments.body:
The pattern is easier to unwind now that we have min/max intrinsics:
https://alive2.llvm.org/ce/z/QmCDqC

I think this is the minimal missing fold:
[e5d15e116292](https://reviews.llvm.org/rGe5d15e116292)
And there's a sibling inverted fold:
[b0bfefb6ecbb](https://reviews.llvm.org/rGb0bfefb6ecbb)

But we need at least one more enhancement/fold to see through the shuffle.
